summary: O'Neil provides a personal update on her progress toward bariatric surgery, having completed all paperwork and awaiting insurance approval. She explains the pre-surgery requirements including a two-week protein-shake-only diet designed to shrink her fatty liver for the laparoscopic procedure. The post details the four stages of post-surgery dietary recovery, from liquid-only intake to eventually eating normal but much smaller portions. She acknowledges the intimidating behavioral changes required but expresses hope based on others' experiences that reduced hunger will make the transition manageable.
key_points: A strict two-week pre-surgery starvation diet is required to reduce liver size, as 'non-alcoholic fatty liver' is common in heavier patients and can interfere with surgical tools
Post-surgery recovery involves four distinct dietary phases, starting with 1-ounce liquid intake every 20 minutes and gradually progressing to normal food in small portions
Long-term lifestyle rules include never eating and drinking simultaneously, avoiding carbonated beverages, limiting fat to 25% of diet, and taking vitamins for life
The key selling point from others who've had the surgery: you're not hungry all the time despite eating far less
money_quotes: Of course I do get impatient, mom, but I just hold it inside and I think about positive things like that we'll eventually be home and that we'll be able to see our family
It turns out that many people of my weight have 'non-alcoholic fatty liver,' which just means a liver that's bigger and contains more fat than a normal liver
The good news is that livers respond quickly to dieting, so the two week extreme diet goes pretty far in decreasing the size of the liver to a manageable obstacle
This sounds like a miracle to me, as a person whose hunger rages at me like someone screaming in my ears on a daily basis
I'm taking a leap of faith, knowing that I'm pretty good at following plans I've set for myself, and also knowing that once you've developed a habit, it's not that hard to follow it
key_insight: The promise that makes drastic surgical intervention worthwhile is freedom from constant, overwhelming hunger—a transformation that those who haven't experienced it might not fully appreciate.
